rcaIntro.title: Root cause analysis guide
rcaIntro.description: See what each method is for, when to pick it, and example scenarios.
rcaIntro.whatIsBody: It is a systematic technique to find structural or systemic drivers of an event—not just the surface symptom. Applied well, it prevents the same class of incident from recurring: it treats the disease, not only the symptom. In OHS, Turkish Law 6331 and ISO 45001 require root-cause work for incidents.
whenUse: After a work accident or near miss — often a legal requirement
Recurring non-conformities — the same issue keeps coming back
Proactive review of high-risk processes (fire, chemicals, work at height)
Systemic correction after audits or customer feedback
Suspected occupational disease — long exposure chains

r2d_rca.label: R₂D-RCA
r2d_rca.bestFor: Numeric, repeatable measurement; AI-assisted scoring; continuous monitoring and digital-twin alignment.
r2d_rca.example: Operator interventions on a bench rise over six months; which dimension broke the risk profile? AI produces t₀/t₁ across nine dimensions, compares them, and surfaces a primary root cause when override thresholds are exceeded.

ishikawa.label: Ishikawa (fishbone)
ishikawa.bestFor: Multi-factor events, team brainstorming, visual classification (6M).
ishikawa.example: Product defects rise on a line; map likely causes under people/machine/method/material/measurement/environment and discuss with the team.

five_why.label: 5 Whys
five_why.bestFor: Fast single-focus root-cause checks and simple causal chains.
five_why.example: Hydraulic press leaks oil → Why? Worn seal → Why? No periodic maintenance → Why? No maintenance plan → Root cause: missing maintenance management system.

fault_tree.label: Fault tree (FTA)
fault_tree.bestFor: Complex systems, logical conditions (AND/OR), probability thinking.
fault_tree.example: Explosion risk: top event “fire occurs” with AND/OR gates for spark sources, flammable atmosphere, failed ventilation, etc.

scat.label: SCAT (bird model)
scat.bestFor: Classic OHS incident investigation: immediate → basic → systemic cause chains.
scat.example: After a fall: immediate event (fall), immediate causes (slippery floor), basic causes (training gaps), systemic gaps (no inspection procedure).

bow_tie.label: Bow-tie
bow_tie.bestFor: Hazard + barrier thinking; high-risk work and process safety.
bow_tie.example: Chemical release: threats (valve failure, pressure), preventive barriers (alarms, valve testing), consequences (injury, fire), mitigating barriers (emergency response, sprinklers).

mort.label: MORT
mort.bestFor: Management-system weaknesses; organisational learning after major events.
mort.example: After a large incident: supervisory gaps, policy holes, failed assurance systems, and lessons learned grouped systematically.

tips: Need a quick read → 5 Whys or R₂D-RCA
processSteps: Define the event clearly (company, location, date, who/what/where/how)
Choose the right method (use the tips above)
Generate a first draft with AI or fill manually
Review with the team and close gaps
Confirm root causes → build corrective/preventive (CAPA) actions
Produce a PDF report and share (QR for mobile access)
Follow up: action closure timelines + prevention of recurrence

landing.heroDescription: RiskNova is an AI-powered OHS platform designed to unify risk analysis, field tracking, recording, reporting and decision support processes into a single product experience.
landing.featuresDescription: Professional tools that manage your OHS processes end-to-end.
landing.feat1Desc: A product foundation that collects manual or module-based risk inputs in a single flow and makes results more visible.
landing.feat2Desc: Brings together audit, finding, task and tracking areas in an order that the operations team can read.
landing.feat3Desc: Provides a consistent screen backbone where generated results can be linked to history, detail and output processes.
landing.feat4Desc: Prepares a professional and trust-inspiring foundation for areas such as risk analysis screen, results screen and score history.
landing.feat5Desc: Maintains readability across different devices with single column, two column and wide screen layouts.
landing.feat6Desc: Creates a professional SaaS feel with clean surfaces, controlled contrast and clear CTA structure.
landing.howDescription: Simple steps to get started with our platform.
landing.step1Desc: Sign in securely to the platform with your organization-specific account.
landing.step2Desc: Transfer field audits, hazard reports and risk parameters to the system.
landing.step3Desc: Our AI-powered analysis engine processes your data to calculate risk scores.
landing.step4Desc: Create action plans based on results, track and report.
landing.whyDescription: Creates a professional SaaS experience with blue trust and gold quality emphasis, clean surfaces and soft contrast.
landing.whyBullet1: Clean, modern and trust-inspiring OHS appearance
landing.whyBullet3: Design system ready for Risk Intelligence module
landing.ctaTitle: Turn your risk module into a product showcase
landing.ctaDescription: Manage your OHS processes on a single platform with professional appearance, powerful infrastructure and AI support.
landing.metaTitle: Manage OHS from one platform | RiskNova
landing.metaDescription: AI-powered workplace safety software: risk analysis, field audits, findings, CAPA and reporting in one platform — individual, OSGB and enterprise.
landing.metaOgTitle: RiskNova — Manage OHS from one platform
landing.metaOgDescription: From measurement to audits, actions and reports — run safety processes in one place.
landing.heroSubtitle: From measurement to audit findings, action tracking, reporting and archives — unify processes under one roof. Scales from individuals to OSGBs and multi-site enterprises.
landing.solutionsLeadBold: Are you an OSGB or a multi-site organisation?
landing.solutionsLeadRest: Review plans, leave a lead via quote or signup — our team will follow up.
landing.valueTryTitle: Risk, audits and reporting in one product experience
landing.valueTryDesc: Forms, results, score history and outputs use consistent screens — your team keeps operating without friction.
landing.testimonial1Quote: Field audits became much more systematic with RiskNova. The AI commentary is genuinely helpful.
landing.testimonial2Quote: Having risk analysis, reporting and tracking in one platform improved our operational efficiency.
landing.testimonial3Quote: Corporate look and ease of use meant the team adopted it very quickly.
landing.bottomCtaDescription: Manage all your workplace safety operations from risk analysis to reporting on a single platform with RiskNova.
landing.footerBlurb: AI-powered OHS risk analysis and operations platform — professional, reliable and modern.
landing.subscriptionDisclaimer: RiskNova provides subscription-based workplace health and safety software.

pricing.heroTitle: Clear, scalable plans for workplace safety professionals.
pricing.heroDescription: Start on Free and grow through Starter, Plus and Professional — Nova, risk analysis, field audits and library-backed document generation scale with your pace. On paid plans you choose monthly or yearly billing on each card.
pricing.introBody: On paid plans you can pick monthly or yearly billing on each card. Yearly billing covers twelve months for the price of ten (the card total updates accordingly). OSGB and enterprise customers use the quote flow. Each card shows “Who is it for?” and “Move up a tier” so you can see the next step for your profile.
pricing.priceMonthlyNote: Monthly billing — upgrade whenever you need
pricing.footerLimitsBody: Nova, analysis and generation allowances are consumed at the API layer — not only on screen.
pricing.footerOsgbBody: Team seats, portfolios, white-label, onboarding and invoicing are handled through the dedicated quote flow.
pricing.footerPro149Body: Balanced capacity for analyses, documents and field work for active individual professionals.
pricing.enterpriseBody: For multiple users, client portfolios, white-label, bespoke onboarding and invoicing there is no self‑serve checkout on this path — contact us to agree scope and contract.

free.name: Free
free.whoFor: Trying RiskNova, students and light occasional use.
free.description: Entry tier with monthly quotas to explore Nova chat, analyses and a small document workflow.
free.upgradeHint: Upgrade to Starter when you need predictable quotas every month.
features: Foundational AI analyses for risk workflows

starter.name: Starter
starter.whoFor: Freelance specialists and steady but modest monthly workloads.
starter.description: Higher quotas for Nova, analyses and documents so weekly routines run without friction.
starter.upgradeHint: Choose Plus when incident analysis and training outputs become part of your workflow.
features: Training slides where included in quota

plus.name: Plus
plus.whoFor: Active consultants managing several clients each month.
plus.description: Balanced capacity across chat, analyses, risk modelling and field data.
plus.upgradeHint: Move to Professional when you need deeper inspection and reporting throughput.

Field inspection headroom for busy weeks

privacy.title: RiskNova Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDescription: RiskNova privacy policy: how we process personal data across accounts, product use, billing, email, security and AI-assisted features.
privacy.ogDescription: How RiskNova processes personal data, which processors we use and how we support user rights.

sections.title: Data controller and scope
sections.content: This policy describes how personal data is processed across the RiskNova website, registration, sign-in, subscription, OHS modules, document generation, field inspections, training/exams/surveys, Nova AI and support. For workplace and employee records uploaded by the customer to the platform, RiskNova generally acts as a processor; for its own account, security, billing and support processes it acts as a controller.

sections.title: Categories of personal data
sections.content: Name, surname, email, phone, account identifier, organisation and workspace details, role and permission records, session and security logs, usage metrics, support tickets, subscription and payment status, IP and device data may be processed. If users upload them, risk analyses, inspections, incident logs, training, exams, surveys, documents, personnel records and media or data files may also be stored on the platform.

sections.title: Purposes and legal bases
sections.content: Data are processed to perform the contract, deliver platform features, run occupational safety processes, authenticate users, prevent abuse, bill and support customers, meet legal obligations, improve the product and respect user choices. Where explicit consent is required, a separate consent text is shown; core service data may rely on contract, legal obligation or legitimate interest instead of consent.

sections.title: AI-assisted features
sections.content: Nova AI may process user inputs and related context when producing risk analyses, document drafts, training content or legislation-aware answers. Outputs are decision-support only; final professional and legal judgement remains with the user. Where content may include sensitive or special-category data, explicit consent and/or additional disclosure flows may be required before sending it to AI services.

sections.title: Third-party processors
sections.content: Hosting, database, authentication, email, payment, error monitoring and AI features may use providers such as Supabase, Vercel, Resend, Paddle, OpenAI, Anthropic and similar vendors. They process data only as needed to provide the service. Card data are not stored by RiskNova; payments and invoicing run on Paddle infrastructure.

sections.title: International transfers
sections.content: Cloud hosting, email, payment and AI services may technically run on infrastructure located abroad, so some data may be transferred to providers outside your country where the service requires it. Where transfers apply, disclosure and consent steps under applicable law (including KVKK where relevant) are managed in the product.

sections.title: Cookies and similar technologies
sections.content: RiskNova uses essential session, security, language preference and product cookies. If marketing or third-party tracking cookies are introduced, a separate cookie policy and consent mechanism will be provided as needed. Details are on the /cookie-policy page.

sections.title: Retention and security
sections.content: Data are kept for as long as needed to operate the service, meet contractual, security, legal and evidentiary requirements. The platform uses technical measures such as RLS, role checks, separation of service roles, webhook signature verification, rate limiting, audit trails and security event logging.

sections.title: Your rights
sections.content: Under applicable data-protection law (including KVKK Article 11 where it applies), you may request access, rectification, erasure, objection, restriction, portability and information via the Privacy / Data Rights area in your profile or at support@getrisknova.com. Erasure requests are balanced against legal retention and security logging requirements.

sections.title: Contact and updates
sections.content: This text may change over time. Material updates may require renewed acknowledgement or notice in the product. For privacy and data-protection requests, contact support@getrisknova.com.

privacySummary.title: RiskNova Privacy Policy
privacySummary.metaDescription: Summary of how RiskNova collects and uses personal data.
privacySummary.ogDescription: Short privacy summary covering account, product use and subprocessors.
paragraphs: RiskNova collects account, workspace, billing, and product usage information needed to provide occupational health and safety software services.

We use this information to authenticate users, provide subscribed features, process payments, support customers, improve the product, and maintain security. Payment processing is handled by Paddle; RiskNova does not store full card details.

Users may upload workplace safety content such as risk analysis information, documents, training materials, and inspection notes. This content is used to provide the requested product features and is not sold to third parties.
We use trusted service providers for hosting, authentication, analytics, AI processing, email, and billing. These providers may process data only as necessary to deliver the service.
Users can request access, correction, export, or deletion of their personal data subject to legal, security, and operational retention requirements.
For privacy questions or data requests, contact us through the contact channels listed on the RiskNova website.

terms.title: RiskNova Terms of Use
terms.metaDescription: RiskNova terms of use covering accounts, subscription, billing, acceptable use, AI outputs and customer data responsibilities.
terms.ogDescription: Terms for the RiskNova SaaS platform: subscription, account security, AI outputs and acceptable use.

sections.title: Service scope
sections.content: RiskNova is a subscription SaaS platform for occupational health and safety professionals, OSGBs and enterprises, offering risk analysis, field inspections, document generation, training/exams/surveys, reporting, legislation-aware search and Nova AI decision-support features.

sections.title: Account, roles and security
sections.content: Users are responsible for the accuracy of account details, password strength and actions taken under their account. Organisation administrators must correctly configure users, roles, workspaces and company authorisations. Suspected unauthorised access must be reported to RiskNova without delay.

sections.title: Acceptable use
sections.content: The platform may be used only for lawful, professional and workplace-safety-related purposes. Attempting unauthorised access, reverse engineering, disrupting the service, uploading harmful content, infringing third-party rights, circumventing quotas or processing data unlawfully is prohibited.

sections.title: Customer data and content responsibility
sections.content: Risk analyses, inspection records, documents, personnel data, training/exam/survey content and uploaded files are customer content. Users must ensure data uploaded to the platform is lawful, necessary and limited to authorised persons.

sections.title: AI-assisted outputs
sections.content: Nova AI and other AI features provide decision support and drafts only. RiskNova does not warrant that outputs are error-free, complete or fit for a particular legal or technical outcome. Users must validate outputs against applicable law, internal procedures and professional judgement.

sections.title: Subscription, billing and limits
sections.content: Plan features, fees, usage limits and billing periods are shown on pricing or checkout screens. Individual plans may use self-service checkout; OSGB and enterprise models may follow quote and contact flows. Payments and invoicing may be handled by Paddle as Merchant of Record.

sections.title: Service changes and access
sections.content: RiskNova may update product features, plan limits, integrations and these terms over time. Access may be temporarily restricted for security, maintenance, legal compliance or abuse prevention.

sections.title: Limitation of responsibility
sections.content: The platform is designed to streamline processes; it does not remove legal duties of employers, safety professionals or organisations. Users remain responsible for field, regulatory and expert review before relying on platform outputs.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: For questions about these terms, subscriptions or accounts, contact support@getrisknova.com.

termsSummary.title: RiskNova Terms and Conditions
termsSummary.metaDescription: Summary of RiskNova terms of service, acceptable use and subscriptions.
termsSummary.ogDescription: Summary of subscription, AI-assisted features and acceptable use.
paragraphs: RiskNova is a subscription-based SaaS platform for occupational health and safety professionals. By creating an account or using RiskNova, you agree to use the service only for lawful professional safety, documentation, analysis, and workflow management purposes.
Individual plans are intended for self-service professional use. OSGB and corporate use cases may require a separate commercial agreement and onboarding process.

RiskNova may include AI-assisted analysis and document generation. Users remain responsible for reviewing outputs, applying their own professional judgment, and complying with applicable laws, standards, and workplace procedures.

Subscription access, limits, billing cycles, and available features are shown on the pricing page before checkout. We may update plans or features over time, but active subscriptions will continue under the terms presented at purchase unless otherwise communicated.
Users must not misuse the service, attempt unauthorized access, upload unlawful content, or use RiskNova to replace required legal, medical, engineering, or regulatory professional review.
For questions about these terms, contact us through the contact channels listed on the RiskNova website.

refund.title: RiskNova Refund Policy
refund.metaDescription: How RiskNova handles subscription refunds, renewals and Paddle billing rules.
refund.ogDescription: Refund window, renewals and billing corrections.
paragraphs: RiskNova subscriptions are billed monthly or annually according to the plan selected at checkout.
Customers may request a refund within 14 days of the initial purchase if the service has not been materially used and the request is not abusive or fraudulent.
Renewal payments are generally non-refundable once a new billing period has started. Customers can cancel future renewals from their account or by contacting support.
If a billing error, duplicate charge, or unauthorized transaction is confirmed, we will work with Paddle to correct the issue.
Refund decisions may depend on usage, subscription type, legal requirements, and Paddle billing rules. Approved refunds are returned to the original payment method through Paddle.
For refund requests, contact us through the contact channels listed on the RiskNova website and include the account email and order details.

cookiePolicy.title: RiskNova Cookie Policy
cookiePolicy.metaDescription: How RiskNova uses essential session, security and preference cookies and similar technologies.
cookiePolicy.ogDescription: Essential cookies for sign-in, security and preferences; analytics and marketing cookies are not part of the core flow today.

sections.title: What are cookies?
sections.content: Cookies and similar local storage technologies are small technical records that help the web application remember session, security, language preference and product functions.

sections.title: Essential cookies
sections.content: RiskNova uses essential cookies for maintaining your session, the Supabase authentication flow, OAuth redirects, password reset, security checks, CSRF/flow consistency and language preference. If these cookies are disabled, core platform features may not work.

sections.title: Preference and product cookies
sections.content: Language, region, UI preference, last selected workspace or similar product settings may be stored in cookies or localStorage to keep the experience consistent.

sections.title: Analytics and marketing cookies
sections.content: This release does not rely on third-party analytics or marketing cookies as part of mandatory flows. If analytics or marketing cookies are introduced later, users will receive separate notice and consent controls where required.

sections.title: Managing cookies
sections.content: You can delete or block cookies from your browser settings. Blocking essential session, security or language cookies may break sign-in, registration, billing or in-app workflows.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: For questions about cookies or privacy settings, email support@getrisknova.com.

pwaInstallPage.metaDescription: Install RiskNova as a PWA on iOS, Android, and Windows without an app store.
pwaInstallPage.heroTitle: Use RiskNova like an app on mobile and desktop
pwaInstallPage.heroBody: Add RiskNova to your home screen or desktop on iOS, Android, and Windows. Updates to the site roll out to your installed experience automatically.
pwaInstallPage.updatesBody: When the RiskNova web app is updated, your PWA picks up the new version—you don't wait on store updates.

android.title: Android
android.text: Open RiskNova in Chrome or Edge; if you see an install prompt, choose Install app.
android.step3: Sign in from the RiskNova icon

ios.title: iPhone & iPad
ios.text: Open in Safari; use Share → Add to Home Screen to create the RiskNova icon.

windows.title: Windows
windows.text: Use the install icon in the Chrome or Edge address bar to open RiskNova in its own desktop window.
windows.step3: Open RiskNova from the Start menu

pwaPrompt.publicDescription: Open quickly in a separate window on iOS, Android, and Windows.
pwaPrompt.appDescription: Jump back to field work, documents, and Nova from your home screen.
pwaPrompt.iosHint: On iPhone/iPad, use Share → Add to Home Screen.

follow_up.label: Acceptable
follow_up.action: May need improvement suggestions; no urgent action

medium.label: Significant
medium.action: Take action in the short term

follow_up.label: Acceptable
follow_up.action: Further measures may not be needed; monitoring sufficient

critical.label: Critical risk
critical.action: Stop work; perform root cause analysis

high.label: High
high.action: Process change or extra barrier required

critical.label: Critical
critical.action: Do not operate; eliminate root causes

low.label: Acceptable
low.action: Close gaps in the short term

high.label: High risk
high.action: Multiple layers or process change required

c1.label: Hazard density
c1.description: Count / density of hazardous objects observed in the field

c3.label: Behaviour risk
c3.description: Restricted zone entry, unguarded work, fall exposure

c4.label: Environmental stress
c4.description: Heat, noise, vibration and similar stressors

c6.label: Access / obstruction
c6.description: Blocked exits, wet floors, passage obstacles

c8.label: Vehicle traffic
c8.description: Forklift intensity, line crossings, pedestrian–vehicle conflict

r_skor.name: R-SKOR 2D
r_skor.description: Weighted field screening score with 9 parameters
r_skor.tooltip: RiskNova method: nine parameters (C1–C9) weight visual and field inputs; supports automated scoring from imagery.

fine_kinney.name: Fine-Kinney
fine_kinney.tooltip: Common quantitative risk method: L (0.1–10), S (1–100), E (0.5–10); aligns with ISO 31000.

l_matrix.name: L-type matrix
l_matrix.tooltip: Pick likelihood (1–5) and severity (1–5); read risk from a 25-cell colour matrix (aligned with Turkish OHS practice).

fmea.name: FMEA
fmea.tooltip: Severity (1–10) × Occurrence (1–10) × Detection (1–10) = RPN. Ideal for operations and maintenance; IEC 60812.

hazop.name: HAZOP
hazop.tooltip: Guide words (No, Less, More, Reverse, …) explore process deviations; common in chemical/petro plants; IEC 61882.

bow_tie.name: Bow-Tie
bow_tie.tooltip: Threat likelihood and consequence severity with preventive and mitigative barriers; residual risk from barrier strength.

fta.name: FTA
fta.tooltip: Decompose system failure with AND/OR gates; used in aviation, nuclear and critical systems; IEC 61025.

checklist.name: Checklist
checklist.tooltip: Weighted compliance from OK / Not OK / Partial items—good for audits and inspections.

jsa.name: JSA
jsa.tooltip: Per-step Severity × Likelihood / Control effectiveness; essential for construction and field work.

lopa.name: LOPA
lopa.description: Layer of protection analysis — frequency reduction
lopa.tooltip: Multiply initiating frequency by layer PFD values; often paired with SIL targets in process safety.

page.analysisNotePlaceholder: Each row is a risk topic or non-conformity group. One or more photos can be attached per row.

step4.saveWarning: Results are not saved yet! If you leave without saving, all findings will be lost.
step4.resultsSubtitle: Risk cards stay linked to annotations on the image. Adjust parameters to change the score.

step4.pinBanner: Click the image to add a risk pin
step4.noRiskThisImageTitle: No significant risk on this image
step4.noRiskThisImageBody: The AI found no concrete hazard on this image. You can still add a manual finding.
step4.aiAnalyzingDetail: Nova AI is analyzing each image…
step4.notRealPhotoBody: This image is a drawing, illustration, or synthetic render. Risk analysis only runs on real site photos.
step4.cannotAnalyzeBody: This image is not suitable for risk analysis.
step4.noRiskDetectedBody: The AI did not find a meaningful hazard or non-conformity on this image.
step4.manualPinHint: You can add your own finding with a manual pin.
